_pattern: All cohort + premium modules follow Learn → See → Try → Quiz → Homework per session. Standalone + Pro modules follow the same pattern but author independently.
_source: catalog-v2-proposal.md (canonical v2) · MODULAR_CATALOG_V1.md (Cowork inputs) · curriculum_v2.md · course-brief-ai-agent-builder.md · curriculum-automation-course-v2.md · curriculum-ai-for-work.md

themes.label: Foundations
themes.why: Start here. Mental model, prompting fundamentals, tool selection. Everything else assumes this layer.

themes.label: Image
themes.why: Generate, style-lock, batch, ship images. Cohort spine + tool-specific modules around Nano Banana, MJ, Flux, Ideogram, Recraft.

themes.label: Video
themes.why: Generate, direct, animate, narrate. Cohort spine teaches motion grammar; tool modules go deep on Kling, Seedance, Runway, Pika. Veo 3 + Sora 2 spec'd for May.

themes.label: Brand, Content & Business
themes.why: Strategy, identity, voice, content systems, monetization. The audience-side spine — how the work gets shipped, sold, and seen.

themes.label: Automate
themes.why: Workflows, schedulers, agents, bots, pipelines. AI Agents & Automation is foundations; bot-builder is the shipping-Pro upsell.

themes.label: Build with Claude
themes.why: Claude Skills, Agents, MCP, prompt scaffolding. For creators who want Claude as the orchestrator across other tools.

tiers.label: Free tier
tiers.blurb: Start here. The mental model first.

tiers.label: Cohort tier
tiers.blurb: Six courses. Twelve weeks. One cohort.

tiers.label: Standalone
tiers.blurb: Drop-in mini-classes. Buy what you need.

tiers.label: Premium tier
tiers.blurb: Unlock as you progress through the cohort. Each ships a real artifact.

tiers.label: Pro tier
tiers.blurb: Standalone Pro courses. Each ships a working thing you'd otherwise pay an agency to build.

modules.title: AI Foundations: The Mental Model First
modules.subtitle: RCTF, tool-selection gate, the four moves that make every other module work.

audience.archetype: Complete beginners. No tech background required.

modules.deliverable: A working prompt library (RCTF method) and a tool-selection rubric.
modules.label: Self-paced · gate course
modules.persona: Complete beginners. No tech background required.
modules.skills: Prompt writing (RCTF method), tool selection, iteration, avoiding hallucination, choosing ChatGPT vs. Claude vs. Gemini.
outcomes: Write professional AI prompts using the RCTF method (Role, Context, Task, Format).
Use ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini to solve real daily work problems.
Recognize hallucinations and verify AI output before sharing.
Iterate effectively instead of restarting from scratch.
Choose the right tool (ChatGPT vs. Claude vs. Gemini) for the task.

modules.title: AI for Work: For Non-Technical Professionals
modules.subtitle: ChatGPT, Claude, Gamma, Canva — for managers, government workers, knowledge teams.

audience.archetype: Non-technical professionals in Armenia. Office workers, knowledge workers, managers in firms or government.

modules.deliverable: A daily AI workflow for one real recurring task — measured against time saved.
modules.ownerNotes: Tier locked as cohort-alternate per Ed call (2026-04-29). Sessions briefed but Learn-side bodies not yet authored.
modules.label: Alternate track
modules.persona: Non-technical professionals in Armenia. Office workers, knowledge workers, managers in firms or government.
modules.skills: ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini for daily work, NotebookLM research synthesis, Gamma for presentations, Canva AI design.

modules.title: Research with AI: Pick → Triangulate → Ship
modules.subtitle: Perplexity, NotebookLM, source verification — research workflow as a deliverable.

audience.archetype: Researchers, founders, journalists, analysts.

modules.deliverable: A research output (memo, brief, or report) shipped through the full pick → triangulate → verify → publish loop.
modules.ownerNotes: Lives in portal-content.js as numeric ID 7; v2 catalog adds it to curriculum.json for the first time.
modules.skills: Source picking, triangulation, NotebookLM workflows, citation hygiene.

modules.title: Prompt Craft: RCTF, Iteration, Voice
modules.subtitle: Drop-in for non-cohort buyers who want the prompting fundamentals on their own.

audience.archetype: Standalone buyers who want the foundations layer without the full cohort.

modules.deliverable: A personal prompt library + iteration playbook.
modules.ownerNotes: Distillation of foundations module for non-cohort audience.

modules.title: AI Image Mastery
modules.subtitle: The umbrella that compares tools and teaches consistency across them.

Understand composition, lighting, and mood in AI-generated images.
Build a personal style library (colors, mood, aesthetic patterns you use repeatedly).
Iterate on images without losing quality.
Compare Flux, Nano Banana, and Midjourney for different use cases.

modules.title: AI Video Creation

modules.skills: Storyboarding, video prompt writing, character consistency, shot sequencing.

Write video prompts that generate usable first drafts.
Keep characters/subjects consistent across 20+ AI-generated frames.
Blend AI video clips with music/narration using free tools.
Deliver broadcast-ready video in under 90 minutes.

Build a design system (colors, typography, composition rules) in Figma.
Create a constraint document that AI generators can follow consistently.
Adapt a brand system for dark/light modes, mobile, and different platforms.
Document your design decisions so a team member can maintain them.

Produce 4 weeks of social media content in under 5 hours using AI drafts.
Write SEO-optimized blog posts 60% faster by using AI research + drafting.
Build a content calendar that coordinates across platforms.
Edit AI-written content down to publishable quality in one pass.

Calculate the ROI of an automation and decide whether to build it.
Build a Fiverr/Upwork gig that generates $300–$2,000 per project.
Deliver recurring revenue through AI-enabled services.

modules.title: AI Agents & Automation
modules.subtitle: Trigger → reasoning → action → feedback. The umbrella under which all v1 automation modules sit.

audience.archetype: Both creative freelancers (Anahit) and business owners (Levon). Intermediate, no coding required.

modules.deliverable: One real automation saving ≥30 min/week.
modules.ownerNotes: Fully briefed. Session 1 fully extracted; sessions 2–6 titles + bodies needed for Learn integration.
modules.tagline: Ship a real automation that saves you 30+ minutes per week. No coding required.
modules.persona: Both creative freelancers (Anahit — Yerevan designer) and business owners (Levon — agency owner). Intermediate, no coding required.
modules.description: The mental model behind every AI automation: trigger → reasoning → action → feedback. 90% of useful automations are workflows with one AI node — start there, not with a full agent.
modules.skills: Trigger/reasoning/action/feedback model, Claude Cowork, Zapier/Make, n8n, choosing workflow vs. agent.
modules.capstone: Ship one real automation saving ≥30 min/week.

modules.title: AI Agent Builder — Telegram & Slack
modules.subtitle: Your first working bot in 90 minutes. Phone becomes your AI control panel.

audience.archetype: Both Anahit (Instagram lead screening) and Levon (support email triage). Intermediate, no coding required.

modules.deliverable: A capstone bot handling one real workflow end-to-end.
modules.ownerNotes: 800-line brief. Lowest authoring cost of all ship-first slate items. Was id: ai-agent-builder in v1.
modules.tagline: Ship a working bot in 90 minutes — your phone becomes your AI control panel.
modules.persona: Both Anahit (Instagram lead screening) and Levon (support email triage). Intermediate, no coding required.
modules.description: Bots beat dashboards for ops: always on, context-aware, mobile-first. Build a stateful Telegram or Slack bot connected to Claude or ChatGPT, locked to your user ID.
modules.skills: Telegram BotFather setup, Claude Code + Telegram plugin, n8n bot listeners, user-ID allowlist, stateful bot memory.
modules.capstone: Ship a capstone bot that handles one real workflow end-to-end.
outcomes: Understand why bots beat dashboards for daily operations.
Create a Telegram bot and lock it down with user ID allowlist.
Connect Claude or ChatGPT to your bot via Claude Code plugin OR n8n.
Build a stateful bot with memory (last 10 messages, context, decisions).
Ship a capstone bot that handles one real workflow end-to-end.
Document your bot for future-you and your team.

_archived.title: Production Workflows: From Idea to Deliverable
_archived._archivedReason: Existed in v1 curriculum.json as cohort #04 with no Learn-side authoring. Catalog v2 has no equivalent module — its concerns are folded into content-production (calendar/voice/portfolio) and ai-business (delivery). Preserved here for historical reference; not rendered in v2.
outcomes: Set up a production folder structure that scales from 1 project to 50.
Use Figma + Google Drive as a collaborative AI asset hub.
Batch-process images/videos in under 2 hours using automation.
Create a delivery checklist that prevents quality issues.
Document feedback loops so iteration is fast and repeatable.

_inconsistencies: TIER MODEL: v1 conflated Premium and Pro pricing tiers. v2 splits them: Premium = locked modules unlocked via cohort progress (or premium $99–149 standalone); Pro = standalone paid courses ($79–129). Catalog v2 also adds explicit 'standalone' tier for $19–79 mini-classes that don't fit the cohort/premium/pro framing.
AI-FOR-WORK: tier locked as 'cohort' (alternate-track) per Ed call 2026-04-29. Audience is non-technical professionals; runs in parallel to the Creative cohort.
PREMIUM UNLOCK DATES: unlocksDate values (May 12, June 9, June 23, July 14) are placeholder cohort milestones based on a Spring 2026 timeline. Ed should confirm against the actual cohort calendar.

PRO COURSE SESSION DEPTH: For both Pro courses (ai-automation, bot-builder), only Session 1 fully extracted into sessionsList. Sessions 2–6 (Automation) and Sessions 2–4 (Agent Builder) need brief expansion before Learn integration.

AUTHORED-NOT-LIVE MODULES (◐ status): premium-style, second-brain, voice-automation, delivery-automation, content-calendar, ai-automation, bot-builder, ugc-pipeline, ai-for-work. These have briefs but no Learn-side learn/see/try/quiz/hw bodies. Cowork content-authoring track owns these.

SPEC MODULES (○ status): prompt-craft, image-batch-pipeline, soul-cinema, veo-3-cinematic, sora-2-narrative, shot-grammar, pricing-positioning, portfolio-system, scheduled-tasks, mcp-creative. These need brief expansion before authoring.
BUNDLE PRICING: $299 / $149 placeholder figures pending Ed margin sanity-check.
VIDEO-PRODUCTION RENAMED: legacy v1 id 'video-production' is now 'video-creation' to align with catalog v2. _legacyId field preserves the old slug for any external references.
CONTENT-AT-SCALE RENAMED: legacy v1 id 'content-at-scale' is now 'content-production'. _legacyId preserved.
PRODUCTION-WORKFLOWS DROPPED: legacy v1 cohort #04 (ai-production / Production Workflows) had no Learn-side content and no equivalent in catalog v2. Moved to _archived[]; not rendered in v2 UI.
